opinion.h: DUNSCOMBE v. SAYLE, ADMINISTRATOR. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF FLORIDA. No. 163. Decided October 10, 1960.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h: STATE TAX COMM. OF ARIZONA v. MURRAY CO. OF TEXAS, 364 U.S. 289 (1960)
opinion.p: 364 U.S. 289 STATE TAX COMMISSION OF ARIZONA v. MURRAY COMPANY OF TEXAS, INC. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE SUPREME COURT OF ARIZONA. No. 168. Decided October 10, 1960.
Certiorari granted; judgment vacated; and case remanded for clarification.
Wade Church, Attorney General of Arizona, Leslie C. Hardy, Chief Assistant Attorney General, and Stanley Z. Goodfarb, Assistant Attorney General, for petitioner.
The petition for writ of certiorari is granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded for clarification. Minnesota v. National Tea Co., 309 U.S. 551.
